question_text: A block of wood has a mass of 600 g and a volume of 800 cm³. Water has a density of 1 g/cm³. Will the wood float or sink in water? Explain your answer by calculating the density of the wood.
correct_answer: The wood will float. Density of wood = 600 ÷ 800 = 0.75 g/cm³. Since 0.75 g/cm³ is less than 1 g/cm³ (density of water), the wood is less dense than water and will float.
explanation: To find if something floats, we calculate its density using the formula density = mass ÷ volume. Since the wood's density (0.75 g/cm³) is less than water's density (1 g/cm³), the wood floats because it is less dense and pushes water out of the way. Objects only float if they are less dense than the liquid they're in.
